The policy library home page states it is the sole authoritative source of Victoria University organisational policy documents; policy pages were fetched successfully.
Robots.txt permits the Student AI Hub and news URL fetched in this run for the OpenClaw crawler user agent; disallowed search and account paths were not accessed.
Robots.txt permits the public library guide path for the OpenClaw crawler user agent; the guide was used only for VU-authored support guidance.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found formal Victoria University Policy Library sources for AI governance and academic integrity, plus official VU Student AI Hub and library-guide pages. The news item announcing the hub was documented but rejected as a primary policy source because the hub and policy-library documents provide direct current guidance.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow staged candidate based on formal VU AI Governance and Responsible Use Policy scope text; not canonical.
Approved as a staged security-review candidate because the policy directly says alternative AI tools must be assessed using the AI Risk Classification Model and follow AI Steering Committee guidance.
Approved as a narrow privacy/data-handling candidate tied to clause 39 of the AI policy; not generalized beyond AI training data or outputs involving sensitive or identifiable data.
Approved because the Academic Integrity Policy directly names work derived from a generative AI model without permission or correct acknowledgement as a breach.
Approved as course/assessment-specific guidance; the claim avoids saying all student AI use is always allowed or always prohibited.
Approved as official library guidance about a recommended tool and data-protection caution; it is not treated as a formal procurement list for all university use.

artifacts.title: Victoria University AI policy staged evidence
artifacts.summary: Staged evidence for Victoria University uses official VU Policy Library and library guidance sources. Candidate claims cover the formal AI governance policy scope, alternative AI tool risk assessment, sensitive AI data access, GenAI academic-integrity acknowledgement, course-level assessment guidance, and the VU library guide's Copilot recommendation.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The rejected news item was not used as direct policy evidence.
The Copilot recommendation claim is based on an official library guide and should not be read as a complete enterprise AI tool registry.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
